title: pilosella tenacicaulis
intro: Pilosella tenacicaulis, also known as Rough Hawk's Beard or Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high content of flavonoids and tannins, offering potential health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Pilosella tenacicaulis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and in some cases, it is also used in traditional herbal remedies as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing.
